frameworks.name: decoded neurofeedback (DecNef)
frameworks.one_line: fMRI multivoxel pattern reinforcement biases internal states without awareness.

frameworks.name: metacognition–consciousness link hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: Tests whether discrimination confidence patterns drive conscious detection.

frameworks.name: signal-detection (detection vs discrimination)
frameworks.one_line: Frames the color-detection task with false-alarm metric.

core_question: Are multivoxel patterns coding perceptual (color-discrimination) confidence causally linked to conscious detection?
key_assumptions: Multivoxel patterns for discrimination confidence can be isolated and reinforced via DecNef.
Discrimination confidence and detection share a common metacognitive substrate.
manipulations: DecNef reward contingent on activating high-confidence multivoxel patterns.
Concurrent color-detection task on mostly achromatic stimuli.
key_findings: Occurrences of high color-discrimination confidence voxel patterns coincided with false alarms in detection.
Confidence-pattern activation predicted illusory color reports.
interpretations: Discrimination confidence representations contribute to conscious detection content.
Supports a mechanistic link between metacognition and conscious perception.
